title: lithospermum scabrum
intro: Lithospermum scabrum, also known as the rockrose or gypsum flower, is a flowering plant species native to Europe and Asia. The medicinal properties of Lithospermum scabrum lie in its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antibacterial properties, which are attributed to its alkaloid content, particularly lithospermic acid. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to soothe wounds, reduce inflammation, and combat infections, making it a popular ingredient in traditional medicine for treating skin conditions and injuries. In herbal preparations, Lithospermum scabrum is often used in the form of tinctures, infusions, or ointments to harness its medicinal properties and provide relief from various health issues.
